steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Establish vertex correspondence
steps.content: We are given that $P$ corresponds to $W$, $Q$ corresponds to $X$, and $R$ corresponds to $Y$. Since the quadrilaterals are listed as $PQRS$ and $WXYZ$, vertices must correspond in order. Therefore, $S$ must correspond to $Z$.

steps.title: Use the corresponding angles property
steps.content: In similar polygons, corresponding angles are congruent. Since $S$ corresponds to $Z$, we have $\angle Z = \angle S = 135^{\circ}$. D is correct.
